title: VN — NAICS 221115 Wind Electric Power Generation

sections.title: CFJ (L7)
sections.content:
> Convert kinetic wind energy into grid-compliant, dispatchable electrical energy and inject it into transmission/distribution networks under the governing grid code.

---

sections.title: Industry Snapshot (market facts)
sections.content:
| Fact | Value | Relevance to Mein Solar |
|---|---|---|
| DE grid-scale BESS capacity | 3 GW (2025) → 6 GW (2026) | Doubling market pull |
| DE wind curtailment (2022) | ~8 TWh, €4.25 bn congestion cost | BESS = curtailment-capture instrument |
| Wind+BESS co-loc revenue stack | ~€400 k/MW/yr (higher than solar) | Strongest €/MW business case |
| Inertia services market | Launches Jan 2026, fixed-price | New revenue stream for grid-forming BESS |
| Onshore OEMs (DE) | Nordex 31% of new grid-connected (2025), Enercon, Vestas | Non-Mein-Solar; buys integration services |
| Offshore OEMs | Siemens Gamesa #1, Vestas (MHI Vestas legacy), GE Vernova | Outside Mein Solar scope (4.5 MW ref << TSO-scale) |

---

sections.title: L7 / L6 Hierarchy
sections.content:
```
L7  Wind Electric Power Generation Ecosystem                  [Moore 1993]
├── L6a  Resource Assessment & Site Development                (CORE)
├── L6b  Permitting & Grid Interconnection                     (CORE)
├── L6c  Wind Plant Construction & Installation                (CORE)
├── L6d  Power Generation & Dispatch                           (CORE)
├── L6e  Repowering / Decommissioning                          (CORE)
├── L6H-OM   O&M / Asset Management                            (HORIZONTAL)
├── L6H-GS   Grid Services & Storage Integration               (HORIZONTAL)  ← MEIN SOLAR VN SEGMENT
├── L6H-QA   HSE, Quality & Compliance                         (HORIZONTAL)
└── L6H-IT   SCADA / IT-OT / Cybersecurity                     (HORIZONTAL)
```

### L6 Core Job Families

| Node | Job family (verb + object) | Output types served |
|---|---|---|
| L6a | Quantify wind resource and secure buildable site | Onshore, Offshore, Repower |
| L6b | Obtain BImSchG/WindSeeG permit and DSO/TSO interconnection | All |
| L6c | Erect turbines, cabling, substation, BoP | All |
| L6d | Generate, forecast, trade, and dispatch wind power | All |
| L6e | Repower aged parks or dismantle at end-of-life | Repower only |
| L6H-OM | Maintain availability and lifecycle performance | All |
| **L6H-GS** | **Provide flexibility, storage and grid services to firm wind output** | **All — esp. onshore with curtailment exposure** |
| L6H-QA | Enforce safety, environmental and grid-code conformity | All |
| L6H-IT | Operate SCADA / EMS / cyber-compliant control | All |

---

sections.title: L5 Expansion — Mein Solar's Segment (L6H-GS)
sections.content:
This is where Mein Solar enters. VN must enumerate all sibling L5 alternatives at this level.

```
L6H-GS  Grid Services & Storage Integration
├── L5  Co-located BESS Storage Asset (EPC-delivered)          ← MEIN SOLAR PRIMARY POSITION
├── L5  Standalone Grid-Booster Storage (park-adjacent)        ← MEIN SOLAR SECONDARY POSITION
├── L5  Synchronous Condenser / Flywheel Inertia System
├── L5  Power-to-X / Electrolyzer Offtake (curtailment sink)
├── L5  Virtual Power Plant / Aggregator Platform (software)
├── L5  Curtailment-Based Demand Response (flexible load)
└── L5  Static VAR / STATCOM Reactive Compensation
```

### L5 Alternatives (Mein Solar's level) — sibling analysis

| L5 Alternative | Technology | Est. share of wind-flex spend (DE, 2026) | Confidence | Mein Solar overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Co-located BESS (LFP, EPC) | Li-ion LFP container + PCS + MV | ~55% | MEDIUM (Timera, Fluence) | **PRIMARY — 100%** |
| Standalone grid-booster BESS | Same stack, park-adjacent | ~20% | MEDIUM | **SECONDARY — 95%** |
| Synchronous condenser / flywheel | Rotating mass | ~8% | LOW | 30% (MV coupling only) |
| P2X / electrolyzer offtake | PEM/alkaline | ~7% | LOW | 40% (grid-side scope) |
| VPP / aggregator | Software layer | ~5% | LOW | 15% (EMS adjacency) |
| Flex load / DR | Process-side | ~3% | LOW | 5% |
| STATCOM | Power electronics | ~2% | LOW | 20% |
| **Sum** | | **100%** | | |

---

sections.title: L4 Subsystems — under Co-located BESS L5 (Mein Solar primary)
sections.content:
INCOSE-compliant subsystems. Minimum 3 required; structural gate = ≥2.

| L4 Subsystem | Function | Typical vendor |
|---|---|---|
| LFP Battery Container | Electrochemical storage, BMS, HVAC, fire suppression | CATL EnerC, Sungrow PowerTitan, Tesla Megapack, SolarEast |
| Bidirectional PCS | DC↔AC, 4-quadrant, grid-forming firmware | SMA, Sungrow, Power Electronics, Ingeteam |
| MV Transformer & Switchgear | Step-up to wind park 20/30 kV collector | Siemens, ABB, Schneider, Eaton |
| Protection & NA-Schutz Relays | VDE-AR-N 4110/4120 compliance | Siemens SIPROTEC, ABB REF/REL |
| Energy Management System | Curtailment, arbitrage, FCR/aFRR/inertia dispatch | Entrix, Next Kraftwerke, Fluence Mosaic, in-house |
| Civil Works & Foundation | Pads, trenching, fencing, Feuerwehr access | Local subcontractors |
| Grid-Code Certification Dossier | FGW TR3 unit + TR8 plant cert | TÜV, DEKRA, 8.2 Consulting |

### L4 under other key L5 siblings (for gate compliance)

**Synchronous Condenser:** rotor+stator, excitation system, cooling skid, MV breaker.
**Electrolyzer offtake:** stack module, gas cleanup, H2 compressor, PCS rectifier.
**VPP platform:** forecasting engine, market-API gateway, asset-control layer.

---

sections.title: L6 Core Process Expansion (context for adjacency scans)
sections.content:
| L6 | Core L5 positions (named, unexpanded — Mein Solar has no primary here) |
|---|---|
| L6a | Met-mast / LiDAR wind measurement · Layout & yield software (WAsP, WindPRO) · Site surveying · Environmental impact study |
| L6b | BImSchG permit filing · DSO/TSO interconnection application · Land lease contracting · Grid-code pre-certification |
| L6c | Onshore wind turbine (Nordex, Enercon, Vestas, GE) · Offshore turbine (SGRE, Vestas) · Tower & foundation · Array cabling · Park substation · Collector system · SCADA cabinet · BoP civil |
| L6d | Wind forecast service · PPA/market trading · Direct-marketing aggregator · Dispatch scheduler |
| L6e | Crane & dismantling services · Blade recycling · Refurbishment |
| L6H-OM | Turbine OEM service · ISP (Deutsche Windtechnik, Availon) · Drone blade inspection · Gearbox overhaul · Spare parts |
| L6H-QA | BG-accredited safety · Bat/bird monitoring · FGW certifier · Grid-code auditor |
| L6H-IT | SCADA (Bachmann, Mita-Teknik) · Cybersecurity (BSI IT-Grundschutz, IEC 62443) |

---

sections.content:
```

    L5  Co-located BESS Storage Asset (EPC-delivered)    ← MEIN SOLAR PRIMARY POSITION
    L5  Standalone Grid-Booster Storage                  ← MEIN SOLAR SECONDARY POSITION
    L5  Synchronous Condenser / Flywheel

```

---

sections.title: 04e — Direct Competence Transfer Scan (cross-VN)
sections.content:
| VN Unit | Level | Competence Required | Has It? | Effort | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Standalone Grid-Booster Storage | L5 (L6H-GS) | Same as co-located, park-adjacent siting | YES | LOW | Identical stack; site logic only differs |
| Park Substation (MV collector) | L5 (L6c) | MV transformer + switchgear EPC | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | MS already does MV for BESS; wind-park collector is same equipment |
| Array Cabling (33 kV) | L5 (L6c) | MV cable install, trenching, terminations | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | Civil + cabling heritage from PV farm EPC |
| DSO/TSO Interconnection app. | L5 (L6b) | VDE-AR-N 4110/4120 filing + FGW TR8 | YES | LOW | Core capability — already done for BESS |
| Grid-code certification dossier | L4 (cross) | FGW TR3/TR8 package | YES | LOW | Native capability |
| EMS / dispatch platform | L5 (L6H-GS) | Optimization, market APIs, SoC mgmt | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | Has BESS EMS; VPP/aggregator-grade is gap |

(HIGH-effort units — turbine supply, blade recycling, offshore — excluded.)

---

sections.title: 04f — Strategic Adjacency Expansion (within L6H-GS segment)
sections.content:
| Adjacent L5 | Current Incumbents | Capability Gap | Investment | Fit (1–10) | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Standalone Grid-Booster Storage | Fluence, Tesla, Sungrow, Wärtsilä | Project-finance structuring at park scale | 6 mo, <€200k | **9** | ~100% stack overlap; only siting/commercial model differs |
| Synchronous Condenser / Flywheel | Hitachi Energy, ABB, Siemens Energy | Rotating-machine engineering, exciter controls | 18–24 mo, €3–5 M | 3 | <30% overlap; exit not worth it |
| P2X / Electrolyzer Offtake | Nel, Sunfire, Siemens Energy, ITM | Stack engineering, H2 safety, gas balance of plant | 24+ mo, >€5 M | 3 | Electrical side transfers; H2 chemistry does not |
| VPP / Aggregator | Next Kraftwerke, Entrix, Flexpower | Market-trading SW, algo-dispatch IP, balancing-group entity | 12–18 mo, €2 M + license | 5 | Platform play — partner, don't build |
| STATCOM | ABB, Siemens Energy, GE Vernova | HV power-electronics engineering | >24 mo, >€5 M | 2 | *excluded — <30% overlap* |

**Recommendation:** Only **Standalone Grid-Booster Storage** clears the 30% overlap + fit threshold as an organic adjacency. VPP is a partnership candidate, not a build.

---

sections.title: 04g — Vertical Move-Up Assessment (L5 → L6)
sections.content:
| Current | Target | What must be added | Competitors at target | Feasibility | Economic rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| L5 Co-located BESS (under L6H-GS) | L6H-GS *Grid Services & Storage Integration* — own the whole flex layer for a wind park | VPP/market-trading platform, inertia-service cert, balancing-group license, 24/7 dispatch desk | Next Kraftwerke, Entrix, Fluence Digital, Flexpower, RWE Supply & Trading | **LOW** | L5→L6H requires ops + trading + balancing-group org fundamentally different from EPC project business. TAM at L6H (~€400 k/MW × DE wind 70 GW ≈ €28 B exposure) is attractive but move-up is not organic — acquisition or partnership is the rational path. |
| L5 Co-located BESS | L6c Wind Plant Construction (wind-park BoP EPC) | Turbine erection competence, crane logistics, blade handling, offshore marine (if applicable) | Max Bögl, Goldbeck, BayWa r.e., Nordex-EPC | **LOW** | Turbine erection is specialist trade; MS has no crane fleet or offshore vessels. Cross-segment move; viable only as BoP subcontractor role. |

**If LOW (primary move-up):** The pivot from project-delivery EPC to 24/7 dispatch/trading requires a different balance sheet (prop trading capital, collateral), a different regulatory position (BNetzA balancing-group registration), and a different org (desk, risk, IT). Organic build is not economical within 18-month horizons.

---

sections.title: VN Completeness Gate
sections.content:
| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| Q1 CFJ industry-level, mechanism-free | ✅ |
| Q2 No orphan nodes | ✅ |
| Q3 Valid level transitions | ✅ (L7→L6→L5→L4) |
| Q4 Every L5 has ≥2 L4 children | ✅ (primary L5 has 7) |
| Q5 No solution-specific L7/L6 names | ✅ |
| Q6 Naming test | ✅ (BESS, PCS, STATCOM are industry terms) |
| Q7 Unit count 15–60 | ✅ (≈ 35 units) |
| Full depth to company position | ✅ (L7 → L6H-GS → L5 BESS → L4 subsystems) |
| Sibling L5 enumerated at company level | ✅ (7 alternatives) |

---

sections.title: Strategic Takeaways
sections.content:
1. **Mein Solar's fit in 221115 is conditional on co-location role.** The VN entry point is *not* turbine/park EPC (that is owned by specialists) — it is the **L6H-GS Grid-Services layer** attached to new or repowered wind parks.
2. **Strongest adjacency is "same L5, different siting"** (standalone grid-booster). This is a 6-month organic move — no technology change, only project-finance packaging.
3. **Inertia services (Jan 2026) and curtailment capture are the anchor revenue streams.** Mein Solar must add grid-forming PCS firmware + FGW TR3 grid-forming cert to the BESS stack.
4. **Vertical move-up to own L6H-GS is LOW feasibility organically** — requires a trading/dispatch org the company does not have. Partner with an aggregator (Next Kraftwerke, Entrix) instead of building.
5. **Scale mismatch with TSO-grade storage (L6e/transmission, 100–500 MW) is unchanged** — Mein Solar's 4.5 MW / 9 MWh reference fits onshore-park scale, not transmission-grid boosters.

---

TAM ~EUR 28 B (DE wind 70 GW x EUR 400 k/MW revenue stack) but organic build not viable in 18-month horizon. Partnership or acquisition is the rational path."},
    {"current_position": "L5 Co-located BESS (under L6H-GS)", "target_position": "L6c Wind Plant Construction", "capabilities_to_add": ["Turbine erection competence", "Crane logistics", "Blade handling", "Offshore marine (if applicable)"], "competitors_at_target": ["Max Bögl", "Goldbeck", "BayWa r.e.", "Nordex-EPC"], "feasibility": "LOW", "economic_rationale": "Turbine erection is specialist trade; Mein Solar has no crane fleet or marine assets. Viable only as BoP subcontractor role, not full wind-park EPC integrator."}

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
- [NAICS 221115 Wind Electric Power Generation (IBISWorld)](https://www.ibisworld.com/classifications/naics/221115/wind-electric-power-generation/)
- [BESS co-location resurgence in Germany (Timera Energy)](https://timera-energy.com/blog/bess-co-location-resurgence-in-germany/)
- [Co-location BESS for Wind and Solar — Economic Analysis (AssetPhysics)](https://assetphysics.com/co-location-bess-for-wind-and-solar-economic-analysis-and-financing-solutions/)
- [Germany to Launch Inertia Service Market in 2026 (HSF Kramer)](https://www.hsfkramer.com/notes/energy/2025-posts/germany-to-launch-inertia-service-market-in-2026-new-revenue-stream-for-grid-forming-bess)
- [Germany's Innovation Tender — Renewable + Storage Co-location (Fluence)](https://blog.fluenceenergy.com/germany-innovation-tender-full-potential-renewable-storage-co-location)
- [Revenue potential in the German BESS market (Rabobank)](https://www.rabobank.com/knowledge/d011485293-backup-power-for-europe-part-5-revenue-potential-in-the-german-bess-market)
- [Global Top 15 Wind Turbine Manufacturers 2026 (Blackridge Research)](https://www.blackridgeresearch.com/blog/top-wind-turbine-manufacturers-makers-companies-suppliers)
- [Wind Turbines Manufacturers Market Share (Enerdata)](https://www.enerdata.net/publications/executive-briefing/wind-market-share.html)

rows.Relevance to Mein Solar: New revenue stream for grid-forming BESS

rows.Value: Nordex 31% of new grid-connected (2025), Enercon, Vestas
Siemens Gamesa #1, Vestas (MHI Vestas legacy), GE Vernova
rows.Relevance to Mein Solar: Outside Mein Solar scope (4.5 MW ref << TSO-scale)

rows.Job family (verb + object): Quantify wind resource and secure buildable site
Obtain BImSchG/WindSeeG permit and DSO/TSO interconnection
Generate, forecast, trade, and dispatch wind power
Repower aged parks or dismantle at end-of-life
**Provide flexibility, storage and grid services to firm wind output**
Enforce safety, environmental and grid-code conformity

rows.Function: Electrochemical storage, BMS, HVAC, fire suppression
rows.Typical vendor: CATL EnerC, Sungrow PowerTitan, Tesla Megapack, SolarEast

rows.Function: Step-up to wind park 20/30 kV collector

rows.Typical vendor: Entrix, Next Kraftwerke, Fluence Mosaic, in-house

rows.Function: FGW TR3 unit + TR8 plant cert

headers: Core L5 positions (named, unexpanded — Mein Solar has no primary here)

rows.Core L5 positions (named, unexpanded — M: Met-mast / LiDAR wind measurement · Layout & yield software (WAsP, WindPRO) · Site surveying · Environmental impact study
BImSchG permit filing · DSO/TSO interconnection application · Land lease contracting · Grid-code pre-certification
Onshore wind turbine (Nordex, Enercon, Vestas, GE) · Offshore turbine (SGRE, Vestas) · Tower & foundation · Array cabling · Park substation · Collector system · SCADA cabinet · BoP civil
Wind forecast service · PPA/market trading · Direct-marketing aggregator · Dispatch scheduler
Crane & dismantling services · Blade recycling · Refurbishment
Turbine OEM service · ISP (Deutsche Windtechnik, Availon) · Drone blade inspection · Gearbox overhaul · Spare parts
BG-accredited safety · Bat/bird monitoring · FGW certifier · Grid-code auditor

rows.Rationale: Identical stack; site logic only differs
MS already does MV for BESS; wind-park collector is same equipment
Civil + cabling heritage from PV farm EPC
Core capability — already done for BESS
Has BESS EMS; VPP/aggregator-grade is gap
~100% stack overlap; only siting/commercial model differs

rows.Capability Gap: Stack engineering, H2 safety, gas balance of plant
rows.Rationale: Electrical side transfers; H2 chemistry does not

rows.Capability Gap: Market-trading SW, algo-dispatch IP, balancing-group entity

rows.Target: L6H-GS *Grid Services & Storage Integration* — own the whole flex layer for a wind park
rows.What must be added: VPP/market-trading platform, inertia-service cert, balancing-group license, 24/7 dispatch desk
rows.Competitors at target: Next Kraftwerke, Entrix, Fluence Digital, Flexpower, RWE Supply & Trading
rows.Economic rationale: L5→L6H requires ops + trading + balancing-group org fundamentally different from EPC project business. TAM at L6H (~€400 k/MW × DE wind 70 GW ≈ €28 B exposure) is attractive but move-up is not organic — acquisition or partnership is the rational path.

rows.Target: L6c Wind Plant Construction (wind-park BoP EPC)
rows.What must be added: Turbine erection competence, crane logistics, blade handling, offshore marine (if applicable)
rows.Competitors at target: Max Bögl, Goldbeck, BayWa r.e., Nordex-EPC
rows.Economic rationale: Turbine erection is specialist trade; MS has no crane fleet or offshore vessels. Cross-segment move; viable only as BoP subcontractor role.

rows.Check: Q4 Every L5 has ≥2 L4 children
Sibling L5 enumerated at company level

entities.cfj_statement: Convert kinetic wind energy into grid-compliant, dispatchable electrical energy and inject it into transmission/distribution networks under the governing grid code.

units.name: Wind Electric Power Generation Ecosystem
units.description: Industry ecosystem converting kinetic wind to grid-injected electrical energy

units.name: Resource Assessment & Site Development
units.description: Quantify wind resource and secure buildable site

units.name: Permitting & Grid Interconnection
units.description: Obtain BImSchG permit and DSO/TSO interconnection

units.name: Wind Plant Construction & Installation
units.description: Erect turbines, foundations, cabling, substation and BoP

units.name: Power Generation & Dispatch
units.description: Generate, forecast, trade and dispatch wind power

units.name: Repowering & Decommissioning
units.description: Repower aged parks or dismantle at end-of-life

units.name: O&M / Asset Management
units.description: Maintain availability and lifecycle performance

units.name: Grid Services & Storage Integration
units.description: Provide flexibility, storage and grid services to firm wind output — MEIN SOLAR VN SEGMENT

units.name: HSE Quality & Compliance
units.description: Enforce safety, environmental and grid-code conformity

units.name: SCADA IT-OT & Cybersecurity
units.description: Operate SCADA, EMS and cyber-compliant control

units.name: Co-located BESS Storage Asset (EPC-delivered)
units.description: Turnkey Li-ion BESS co-located with wind park for curtailment capture and ancillary services

units.name: Standalone Grid-Booster Storage
units.description: Park-adjacent BESS providing grid-boosting and TSO ancillary services

units.name: Synchronous Condenser / Flywheel Inertia System
units.description: Rotating mass providing synthetic inertia and short-circuit contribution

units.name: Power-to-X / Electrolyzer Offtake
units.description: Hydrogen electrolyzer absorbing curtailed wind power

units.name: Virtual Power Plant / Aggregator Platform
units.description: Software layer aggregating distributed wind + storage assets for market dispatch

units.name: Curtailment-Based Demand Response
units.description: Flexible industrial load absorbing surplus wind energy

units.name: Static VAR / STATCOM Reactive Compensation
units.description: Power-electronic reactive-power device for voltage support

units.name: LFP Battery Container
units.description: ISO-container Li-ion LFP rack with BMS, HVAC, fire suppression

units.name: Bidirectional PCS
units.description: 4-quadrant inverter with grid-forming firmware option

units.name: MV Transformer & Switchgear
units.description: Step-up transformer and RMU to 20/30 kV collector

units.name: Protection & NA-Schutz Relays
units.description: Digital protection per VDE-AR-N 4110/4120

units.name: Energy Management System
units.description: Curtailment, arbitrage and FCR/aFRR/inertia dispatch software

units.name: Civil Works & Foundation
units.description: Pads, trenching, fencing, Feuerwehr access

units.name: Grid-Code Certification Dossier
units.description: FGW TR3 unit and TR8 plant certification package

units.name: Grid-Booster BESS Container Block
units.description: BESS container sized for park-adjacent siting

units.name: Grid-Booster PCS Block
units.description: Bidirectional inverter with grid-forming firmware

units.name: Grid-Booster MV Interconnect
units.description: Cable trench and MV switchgear to park collector or DSO feeder

units.name: Synchronous Machine Rotor/Stator
units.description: Rotating electrical machine providing inertia

units.name: Excitation & Cooling System
units.description: Exciter and cooling auxiliaries

units.name: MV Breaker & Protection
units.description: MV switchgear interfacing rotating mass to grid

units.name: Electrolyzer Stack
units.description: PEM or alkaline electrolyzer cell stack

units.name: Hydrogen Balance of Plant
units.description: Gas cleanup, compression, storage

units.name: Rectifier / PCS
units.description: AC-DC rectifier delivering DC to electrolyzer

units.name: Forecasting Engine
units.description: Wind/load forecasting algorithms

units.name: Market API Gateway
units.description: EPEX / Regelleistung.net / FCR prequal APIs

units.name: Asset Control Layer
units.description: Dispatch setpoints to aggregated BESS/wind/flex assets

units.name: Flexible Industrial Load Interface
units.description: Process-side controller modulating load

units.name: Tariff & Settlement Interface
units.description: Metering and settlement platform

units.name: STATCOM Power-Electronic Module
units.description: Converter providing reactive current injection

units.name: STATCOM Controller
units.description: Voltage-support and flicker-mitigation control loop

direct_competence_transfer_04e.rationale: Identical stack and grid-code scope; differs only in siting and project-finance model
Already delivered for BESS; wind-park collector uses same MV equipment
Civil and cabling heritage from PV farm EPC transfers
Has BESS EMS; aggregator/VPP-grade functionality is a gap

strategic_adjacency_04f.rationale: ~100% technology stack overlap; only siting and commercial packaging differ

strategic_adjacency_04f.capability_gap: Stack engineering, H2 safety, gas BoP
strategic_adjacency_04f.rationale: Electrical side transfers; H2 chemistry does not

strategic_adjacency_04f.capability_gap: Trading software, algo-dispatch IP, balancing-group entity
strategic_adjacency_04f.rationale: Partner rather than build — platform economics favor incumbent scale

vertical_move_up_04g.economic_rationale: Requires different balance sheet (trading capital), regulatory position (BNetzA balancing group), and organizational structure (trading desk). TAM ~EUR 28 B (DE wind 70 GW x EUR 400 k/MW revenue stack) but organic build not viable in 18-month horizon. Partnership or acquisition is the rational path.
Turbine erection is specialist trade; Mein Solar has no crane fleet or marine assets. Viable only as BoP subcontractor role, not full wind-park EPC integrator.

industry_facts.mein_solar_scale_match: fits onshore park-co-location (4.5 MW/9 MWh reference); mismatch with TSO transmission-grid boosters (100-500 MW)
